name: pipeline
description: The IR executor: topological parallel scheduling over the desugared DAG, CEL condition evaluation on unrolled conditional chains, run-time generate expansion over user data-source items, failure propagation with skip semantics, and run reporting. The module that runs the reference workflows.

requirements.title: Topological parallel execution
requirements.description: Execute the IR with Kahn-style readiness scheduling: a node becomes eligible when all its edges (data references plus ordering) are satisfied; eligible nodes run concurrently in goroutines; tie-breaking is deterministic (stable name order). The executed graph is the desugared IR — the scheduler never sees loops or sugar.

requirements.title: Conditional steps via CEL
requirements.description: A node's when: condition is a CEL expression over completed step results (steps.X.field) and params. Conditions are compiled at validate time and evaluated at readiness; false means skipped (a distinct terminal state, not failure), and skip propagates through the unrolled loop chains so a settled loop terminates early.

requirements.title: Generate expansion
requirements.description: A generate node invokes its declared data-source command (opaque user command emitting {items: [{id, deps, ...}]}), validates the item shape, and instantiates the named sub-workflow once per item — item fields bound into the instance's params, inter-instance edges derived from each item's deps (edges to items outside the set are ignored). Expansion happens at run time; the expanded subgraph joins the running DAG.

requirements.title: Failure propagation and skip semantics
requirements.description: When a step fails (final, after retries), every transitive dependent is marked skipped with the failed ancestor recorded; independent branches continue to completion. Terminal step states are exactly: ok, failed, skipped (condition false), skipped (dependency failed); deferral is non-terminal — a deferred step later settles into one of the four, its record annotated with the defer history.

requirements.title: Run reporting
requirements.description: After the run settles, aggregate the journal into a run report: per-step status, duration, attempts, key output fields, and per-generate-item rollups, printed human-readably in stable config order and emittable as JSON. The report is derived from the journal, never from in-memory state alone.

requirements.title: Reference workflow execution
requirements.description: The executor runs the reference orchestrator.yaml end-to-end: the task workflow's unrolled review/fix chain settles on the approval condition and its merge step runs only when approved, and the epic workflow's generate node fans out task sub-workflows over the data-source items with dependency-respecting order, each instance settling through its own merge.

requirements.title: Deferred: generate edge cases
requirements.description: Backlog (design edge case 2): generate with an empty item set (no-op vs error, policy flag), malformed data-source output (validation error contract), and dependency cascade policy when one item's sub-workflow fails (skip dependents only vs abort the fan-out). First pass: empty set is a no-op, malformed output fails the generate node, cascade follows standard skip semantics.

requirements.title: Deferred: concurrency control
requirements.description: Backlog (design edge case 5): a scheduler-level parallelism cap aware of host resources (CPU, local GPU) and API rate classes, replacing the first pass's simple max-parallel semaphore.

requirements.title: Deferred: observability
requirements.description: Backlog (design edge case 8): live progress surfaces for a running workflow — step logs and traces, a status view over the journal, and inspection of an in-flight run. First pass: structured slog lines plus the post-run report.

components.name: Scheduler
components.description: Readiness-driven parallel execution over the IR: in-degree tracking, deterministic ready queue, goroutine-per-step with a semaphore cap, terminal-state accounting, failure propagation via dependent traversal, and defer(until) re-queueing. Consults the journal for resume skips and the meter for admission.

components.name: ConditionEvaluator
components.description: CEL environment and lifecycle: validate-time compilation of when: expressions, the steps/params activation built from completed results, and skip decisions at readiness time.

components.name: GenerateExpander
components.description: Run-time fan-out: invokes the data-source command, validates the items contract, instantiates the named sub-workflow per item with param binding and deps-derived edges, and splices the expansion into the running DAG. Owns the deferred empty-set/malformed-source/cascade policies.

components.name: RunReporter
components.description: Aggregates the settled run from the journal into the human summary and JSON report: stable ordering, per-step lines, generate-item rollups, failure diagnostics with handoff pointers. Owns the deferred live-observability seam.

data_flows.name: IR execution flow
data_flows.description: Validated IR + params -> scheduler readiness loop -> (condition check -> journal lookup -> meter admission -> box run -> result record) per step -> generate expansion splices item subgraphs -> journal appends -> settled run -> report.
